# discord_channel (Resource)

Manages a Discord guild channel. Use `discord_category` for category (grouping) channels.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "discord_category" "ops" {
  guild_id = var.guild_id
  name     = "ops"
}

resource "discord_channel" "alerts" {
  guild_id  = var.guild_id
  type      = "text"
  name      = "alerts"
  topic     = "Automated alerts from monitoring systems."
  parent_id = discord_category.ops.id
}

resource "discord_channel" "oncall_voice" {
  guild_id   = var.guild_id
  type       = "voice"
  name       = "oncall"
  parent_id  = discord_category.ops.id
  bitrate    = 64000
  user_limit = 10
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `guild_id` (String) ID of the guild that owns this channel.
- `name` (String) Channel name (1–100 chars).
- `type` (String) Channel type: `text`, `voice`, `news`, `stage`, or `forum`. Not all attributes apply to every type; fields that do not apply are silently ignored by the Discord API.

### Optional

- `bitrate` (Number) Voice bitrate in bits/sec (voice/stage only).
- `nsfw` (Boolean) Whether the channel is marked NSFW (age-restricted).
- `parent_id` (String) ID of the parent category. Null for top-level channels.
- `position` (Number) Sort position within the parent. If omitted, Discord assigns the next available position.
- `rate_limit_per_user` (Number) Per-user slow-mode delay in seconds (text/forum only; 0–21600).
- `topic` (String) Channel topic (text/news/forum only).
- `user_limit` (Number) Max concurrent users (voice/stage only; 0 = unlimited).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Channel ID (snowflake).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import discord_channel.alerts 123456789012345678:987654321098765432
```
